verified.notes: Alaska operates five HCBS waivers (ALI, APDD, CCMC, IDD, ISW). For seniors, the relevant program is the Alaskans Living Independently (ALI) Waiver. Search results confirm eligibility requires Medicaid income/resource limits and nursing facility level of care assessment, but specific dollar thresholds, asset limits, and official application phone number are not provided in the retrieved sources. The Alaska Department of Health official HCBS page (health.alaska.gov) does not include these specific values in the search results. A direct contact with the Division of Public Assistance (DPA) or SDS Intake and Assessment Unit would be required to obtain current 2026 limits and phone numbers.

verified.notes: Income limits derived from 130% Federal Poverty Level threshold stated in search result [5]. Monthly values calculated from annual FPL: $3,381/month for family of 4 implies ~$1,407/month for individual and ~$1,890/month for couple based on standard FPL ratios. Asset limits, phone number, and age-specific eligibility not found in provided search results. Search results confirm SNAP is active in Alaska as of 2026 with federal funding through September 30, 2026 [1]. Official Alaska DPA and USDA SNAP websites would contain complete asset limits and application phone numbers but were not fully indexed in search results provided.
